findings.title: No HttpOnly
findings.message: This looks like a session/token cookie, but JavaScript (and any XSS) can read it.
findings.fix: Add `HttpOnly` unless client-side JS genuinely needs to read the cookie.

findings.title: No Secure
findings.message: Without Secure, the cookie is sent over plaintext HTTP and can be sniffed or MITM'd.
findings.fix: Add `Secure` (and consider the `__Host-` prefix for session cookies).

findings.title: No SameSite
findings.message: Relying on the browser's default (Lax). Set it explicitly — Strict or Lax defends against CSRF.

findings.title: __Host- prefix without Secure
findings.message: A `__Host-` cookie must be Secure, or the browser rejects it (it never gets set).

findings.title: __Host- prefix with a Domain attribute
findings.message: A `__Host-` cookie must NOT set Domain — with it, the browser rejects the cookie.
findings.fix: Remove the Domain attribute (the cookie becomes host-only, which is the point of __Host-).

findings.title: __Host- prefix without Path=/
findings.message: A `__Host-` cookie must set `Path=/` exactly — otherwise the browser rejects it.

findings.title: No HttpOnly
findings.message: This looks like a session/token cookie, but JavaScript (and any XSS) can read it.
findings.fix: Add `HttpOnly` unless client-side JS genuinely needs to read the cookie.

findings.title: No Secure
findings.message: Without Secure, the cookie is sent over plaintext HTTP and can be sniffed or MITM'd.
findings.fix: Add `Secure` (and consider the `__Host-` prefix for session cookies).

findings.title: No SameSite
findings.message: Relying on the browser's default (Lax). Set it explicitly — Strict or Lax defends against CSRF.

findings.title: Domain=example.com shares the cookie with subdomains
findings.message: Setting Domain sends the cookie to every subdomain — including any that's less trusted or attacker-controlled.
findings.fix: Omit Domain for a host-only cookie (the default and most secure).

findings.title: SameSite=None without Secure
findings.message: `SameSite=None` requires `Secure` — without it, modern browsers reject the cookie.
findings.fix: Add `Secure` (or drop SameSite=None if the cookie isn't used cross-site).

findings.title: No HttpOnly
findings.message: This looks like a session/token cookie, but JavaScript (and any XSS) can read it.
findings.fix: Add `HttpOnly` unless client-side JS genuinely needs to read the cookie.

findings.title: No Secure
findings.message: Without Secure, the cookie is sent over plaintext HTTP and can be sniffed or MITM'd.
findings.fix: Add `Secure` (and consider the `__Host-` prefix for session cookies).

findings.title: Lives ~365 days
findings.message: A long-lived auth cookie widens the blast radius if it's ever stolen (and browsers cap at ~400 days anyway).
findings.fix: Shorten the lifetime (≤ 30 days) and refresh sessions instead.

findings.title: No HttpOnly
findings.message: JavaScript can read this cookie; if no client code needs it, add HttpOnly.
findings.fix: Add `HttpOnly` unless client-side JS genuinely needs to read the cookie.

findings.title: No SameSite
findings.message: Relying on the browser's default (Lax). Set it explicitly — Strict or Lax defends against CSRF.
